instructions.name: addShipToFleet
docs: Add ships to a fleet
Logic overview:
- Check that the profile has MANAGE_FLEET permissions
- Check that the star_system and matches the profile faction
- Check that the starbase_player belongs to the profile and is paired with the star_system
- Ensure that the StarbasePlayer and StarSystem accounts are valid
- Ensure that the fleet is docked
- Check that the fleet belongs to the profile and is not destroyed
- Ensure that the fleet is not rented
- Ensure that the fleet has max AP, SP & HP
- Move ships from starbase_player to fleet

instructions.name: cancelCraftingProcess
docs: Cancels a crafting process at a starbase.
Logic overview:
- Check that the ProfileValidation has `SagePermissions::DO_CRAFTING`, with scopes either for the starbase or sage
- Check that the profile belongs to the starbase player
- Check that the starbase player belongs to the starbase
- Check that the starbase seq id matches the crafting process starbase seq id
- if not, the starbase has been destroyed and you must call `destroy_crafting_process` instead
- Check that the crafting process belongs to the starbase player
- Transfer all the inputs from the crafting process's inputs to the starbase player's cargo pod
- Transfer the crew from the crafting process back to the starbase player
- If a crafting hab is required, validate it and then complete the crafting hab job if possible
- Validate the recipe and decrement its usage count
- Close the crafting process account

docs: Cancels local market orders at a starbase and/or withdraws the escrowed funds from any cancelled and all filled orders for
the maker
Logic overview:
- Cancels the specified orders from the instruction (passing in no ids will just withdraw filled escrow funds)
- Remove all filled escrow funds from the maker
- Remove all cancelled order escrowed funds from the maker
- Return the removed cargo or atlas to the starbase player or character vault, respectively
- Remove the makers if they have no open orders
- Return the owed bytes to the profile for the cancelled orders

Logic overview:
- Check that the ProfileValidation has `SagePermissions::DO_STAR_BASE_UPGRADES` with scopes either for the starbase or sage
- Check that the profile belongs to the starbase player
- Check that the starbase player is for the starbase, and that the starbase belongs to the game
- Check that the system has a starbase and that it is upgradeable
- Check that the upgrade process belongs to the profile, starbase player and system
- Check that the starbase seq id matches the upgrade process starbase seq id
- Check that the upgrade process is not complete
- Transfer crew to the starbase player
- Refund resources to the starbase player
- Close the upgrade process account

instructions.name: completeCraftingProcess
docs: Completes a crafting process at a starbase.
Logic overview:
- Check that the ProfileValidation has `SagePermissions::DO_CRAFTING`, with scopes either for the starbase or sage
- Check that the profile belongs to the starbase player
- Check that the starbase player belongs to the starbase
- Check that the starbase belongs to the game
- Check that the recipe is active, is a resource recipe, belongs to the game, and the crafting process is using it
- Check that the crafting process belongs to the starbase player
- Check that the starbase seq id matches the crafting process starbase seq id
- if not, the starbase has been destroyed and you must call `destroy_crafting_process` instead
- Check that the crafting process is complete
- Transfer crew from the crafting process back to the starbase player
- Transfer all the outputs from the recipe to the starbase player's cargo pod
- Validate that the crafting hab is active and then complete the crafting hab job
- Award crafting XP to the character
- Close the crafting process account

instructions.name: createRegion
docs: Creates a new region in the game map.
This instruction creates a new region entry in the region tracker with specified
parameters. It performs authorization checks, ensures the region doesn't already exist,
and initializes it with the provided configuration details.
The region is created with the specified id, owner faction, safety status, and optional
properties such as borders, connections, and research requirements. The provided border
is snapped to the region tracker's grid (`grid_k`), and adjacency (`neighbors_by_edge`)
is rebuilt to reflect shared edges with existing regions. Core systems can be associated
with the region by passing them as remaining accounts.
# Fields
* `id` - Unique identifier for the region
* `owner` - The faction that will own this region (defaults to Unaligned if not provided)
* `safety_status` - The safety level of this region (defaults to Safe if not provided)
* `connections` - Optional connections to other regions by ID
* `border` - Optional region border coordinates
* `scanning_research_requirements` - Optional technology requirements for scanning
* `mining_research_requirements` - Optional technology requirements for mining
* `key_index` - Index of the key in the profile with region management permissions
# System Context
This instruction is part of the spatial management system that enables universe navigation.
It creates the foundation for game mechanics such as exploration, combat zones, and resource acquisition.

accounts.name: systemProgram

instructions.name: createRegionTracker
docs: Creates a new RegionTracker account for storing game map regions.
This instruction initializes a RegionTracker account to store and manage regions
within the game. It performs authorization checks and creates the account with
appropriate funding and initialization parameters.
The account is funded by the provided signer and initialized with a reference
to the relevant game account to maintain the proper relationship between
game state and regions.
# Fields
* `key_index` - Index of the key in the profile with region management permissions
# System Context
This instruction is part of the spatial management system and must be executed before
any region-related operations can be performed. It establishes the storage foundation
for all region data including borders, connections, and ownership.
Grid snapping (`grid_k`)
- The tracker carries a `grid_k` exponent used to snap region borders at admin-time.
- It defaults to `56`, which is a no-op for `Point = I8F56` and preserves full precision.
- Intent: we do not anticipate needing snapping at the beginning; if/when operational
needs arise (e.g., long-term determinism across edits), `grid_k` can be changed in a
future admin instruction/migration before further region creations/updates.
- When `grid_k < 56`, region admin ops snap vertices to a power-of-two grid (`2^-grid_k`),
enabling exact per-edge adjacency via bit-equal endpoints.

instructions.name: deconstructClaimStakeInstance
docs: Implementation of the DeconstructClaimStakeInstance instruction which removes a claim stake instance from a celestial body.
This instruction allows actors to deconstruct an existing `ClaimStakeInstance` from a `CelestialBody`.
The instruction performs the following operations:
- Validates the claim stake instance exists and belongs to the actor
- Ensures the claim stake instance is in active state (not in design)
- Verifies all buildings have been removed before deconstruction
- Removes the claim stake instance from the celestial body's claim stakes list
- Returns the claim stake cargo to the starbase player's cargo pod
- Closes the claim stake instance account to reclaim rent
# Prerequisites
Before deconstructing a claim stake instance:
- All buildings must be removed from the claim stake
- The claim stake must be in active state (not in design phase)
- The actor must have MANAGE_CLAIM_STAKE permissions
# System Context
This instruction is part of the broader claim stake management system and is typically
used when players want to reclaim resources or reorganize their planetary holdings.

instructions.name: destroyCraftingProcess
docs: Destroys a crafting process at a starbase. This is used when the starbase has been destroyed prior to the crafting process completing.
This will close the crafting process, but all inputs will be lost.
Logic overview:
- Check that the crafting process belongs to the profile
- Check that the crafting process belongs to the starbase
- Check that the starbase seq id doesn't match the crafting process starbase seq id
- If a crafting hab is required, validate it and then complete the crafting hab job if possible
- Validate the recipe and decrement its usage count
- Close the crafting process account

- Check that the starbase_player belongs to the star_system

- Remove any ships at the starbase_player and start their respawn process

instructions.name: disbandFleet
docs: Disbands a `Fleet`
Logic overview:
- Check that the profile has MANAGE_FLEET permissions
- Check that the star_system and matches the profile faction
- Check that the starbase_player belongs to the profile and is paired with the star_system
- Ensure that the StarbasePlayer and StarSystem accounts are valid
- Ensure that the fleet is docked

- Ensure that the fleet has max AP, SP & HP
- Ensure the fleet is not on warp or scan cooldown

- Move ships from fleet to starbase_player
- Merge fleet cargo pods with starbase_player cargo pod
- Close the fleet account

instructions.name: evictClaimStakeInstance
docs: Evict a rent-delinquent claim stake instance
Logic overview
- Check that the claim stake instance is placed on the celestial body and belongs
to the profile
- Check that the system which contains the celestial body has an active starbase
and that the profile has a starbase player account on the starbase
- Synchronize rent state first (rent-only; no production backfill) so delinquency is fresh
- Eviction is allowed only when state is `Deactivated` and grace period has elapsed
- Return `EvictionNotDelinquent` if state is still `Active`/`Design`
- Return `CooldownActive` while grace period is still active
- Return buildings and crew to the starbase player account
- Return the claim stake cargo to the starbase player's cargo pod
- Remove the claim stake instance from the celestial body's claim stakes list
- Closes the claim stake instance account to reclaim rent

instructions.name: exitClaimStakesFleetTransfer
docs: Exit cargo transfers between a fleet and a claim stake instance
If the claim stake has been evicted, use [`ForceExitClaimStakesFleetTransfer`](crate::instructions::ForceExitClaimStakesFleetTransfer) instead
Logic overview
- Check that the fleet belongs to the profile, has normalized crew and is not destroyed
- Refresh the fleet state then ensure it is in the claim stake transfer state
- Check that the system which contains the celestial body has an active starbase
and that the profile has a starbase player account on the starbase
- If the claim stake is no longer on the planet then set fleet state to idle
- If the claim stake transfer period is not over then do nothing else
- If the claim stake transfer period has ended then do the cargo transfers and then
set the fleet state to idle

instructions.name: installStarbaseUpgradeResource
docs: Contribute to a starbase upgrade.
Logic overview:
- Check that the ProfileValidation has `SagePermissions::DO_STAR_BASE_UPGRADES` with scopes either for the starbase or sage
- Check that the profile belongs to the starbase player
- Check that the starbase player is for the starbase, and that the starbase belongs to the game
- Check that the system has a starbase and that it is upgradeable
- Check that the upgrade process belongs to the profile, starbase player and system
- Check that the starbase seq id matches the upgrade process starbase seq id
- Check that the upgrade process is complete
- Transfer crew to the starbase player
- Calculate the amount of resource to refund and install
- Refund resources to the starbase player if needed
- Install resources to the starbase upgrade if you can
- If the upgrade is complete, level up the starbase
- Update the system ownership if the starbase started out as unaligned
- TODO: XP/LP?
- Close the upgrade process account

instructions.name: invalidateRental
docs: Invalidates a fleet rental
1. Ensure the caller of the ix is the registered invalidator
2. Ensure rented crew is on board
3. If there is excess crew, those crew must be refunded to the sub profile on record
a. Load and validate the game id
b. Load and validate the sub profile faction
c. Load and validate the starbase and starbase player accounts of the sub profile at their
central space station
d. Add the excess crew to the sub profile starbase player account
4. Reset rented crew to 0
5. If `remove_invalidator` is true
a. Unset the sub_profile and the sub_profile_invalidator
b. Reset rented crew to 0
6. If `remove_invalidator` is false
a. Set the sub_profile to the sage program to flag the fleet as ready to rent

- Validates the claim stake instance exists and belongs to the actor
- Validates the sequence id of the claim stake instance
- Synchronizes rent state first (rent-only; no production backfill)
- Authorizes design mutation policy (`Place` allows `Active|Design`; rejects `Deactivated`)
- Adds or removes the buildings from the claim stake instance
- Increments the sequence id of the claim stake instance

It is typically used after registering a celestial body and when a new claim is being established.

instructions.name: placeCraftingHabBuildings
docs: Implementation of the PlaceCraftingHabBuildings instruction which takes in a list of buildings to add or remove from the crafting hab instance.
This instruction allows actors to add or remove buildings to an existing `CraftingHabInstance` on a `Starbase`.
- Validates the crafting hab instance exists and belongs to the actor
- Validates the sequence id of the crafting hab design
- Synchronizes rent state first (rent-only; no production backfill)
- Authorizes design mutation policy (`Place` allows `Active|Design`; rejects `Deactivated`)
- Adds or removes the buildings from the crafting hab instance
- Increments the sequence id of the crafting hab design

instructions.name: removeRegion
docs: Removes a region from the game map.
This instruction deletes an existing region from the region tracker. It performs
authorization checks and then permanently removes the region with the specified ID
from the game map.
Once a region is removed, any references to it from other game elements (such as
star systems or other connected regions) will become invalid and should be updated
separately.
# Fields
* `id` - Unique identifier for the region to remove
* `key_index` - Index of the key in the profile with region management permissions
# System Context
This instruction is part of the spatial management system that enables universe navigation.
It should be used with caution as it may impact game mechanics that depend on the
existence of the region.

instructions.name: removeShipDefinition
docs: Deletes a ship in `Game`'s [`ShipDefinitions`](crate::state::ShipDefinitions)
# Logic overview:
1. Run the account set validations on [`UpdateShipDefinitionAccounts`]
2. Increment the ship definitions sequence ID (done in `remove_ship_definition`)
3. Delete the ship from `Game`'s [`ShipDefinitions`](crate::state::ShipDefinitions). If the ship id doesn't exist,
return an error.
4. Retain the ship mint in the [mint_to_id](crate::state::ShipDefinitions::mint_to_id) map to allow withdrawal of removed ships.

instructions.name: respawnClaimStakeInstance
docs: Respawn a claim stake instance
Logic overview
- Verify instance ownership and that it is placed on the given celestial body
- Verify it requires respawn (starbase destroyed or level downgrade invalidation)
- Verify `star_system` belongs to the game and matches the celestial body
- Verify `css_star_system` belongs to the game and is a CSS
- Verify `css_starbase_player` belongs to `css_star_system` and the profile
- Remove the instance from the planet, capturing the invalidation timestamp
- Pay rent up to invalidation and refund remaining rent to the character
- Refund claim stake buildings to the CSS starbase player's cargo pod
- Start crew respawn on the system starbase player with the proper end time
- Queue the claim stake respawn on the system starbase player's respawn map
- Close the claim stake instance account

instructions.name: updateRegion
docs: Updates an existing region in the game map.
This instruction modifies properties of an existing region in the region tracker. It
performs authorization checks, ensures the region exists, and updates only the
properties that are explicitly provided in the instruction arguments.
The update can modify any combination of: owner faction, safety status, border,
connections, research requirements, and core systems. Any field not provided
will remain unchanged. When the border changes, it is snapped to the tracker's grid
(`grid_k`) and adjacency (`neighbors_by_edge`) is rebuilt. When connections change,
safety status is updated accordingly.
# Fields
* `id` - Unique identifier for the region to update
* `risk_zone` - Optional new risk zone
* `connections` - Optional new connections to other regions by ID
* `border` - Optional new region border coordinates
* `scanning_research_requirements` - Optional new technology requirements for scanning
* `mining_research_requirements` - Optional new technology requirements for mining
* `xp_modifier` - Optional new XP modifier
* `key_index` - Index of the key in the profile with region management permissions
# System Context
This instruction is part of the spatial management system that enables universe navigation.
It allows for dynamic map changes as the game evolves, affecting gameplay elements such as
faction territory control, safe/unsafe zones, and resource availability.

instructions.name: warpLane
docs: Start warp lane fleet movement
Logic overview:
- Check that the profile has MOVE_FLEET permissions
- Check that the fleet belongs to the profile, has normalized crew and is not destroyed
- Refresh the fleet state then ensure it is idle
- Check that the fleet is within range of the starting warp lane connection
- Validate the provide warp lane connections are connected
- Calculate warp lane distance, duration & expected fuel expenditure
- Check that the warp lane movement will not terminate in an enemy safe zone
- Consume fuel
- Start the warp lane movement

instructions.name: warpToCoordinate
docs: Start warp fleet movement
Logic overview:
- Check that the profile has MOVE_FLEET permissions
- Check that the fleet belongs to the profile, has normalized crew and is not destroyed
- Refresh the fleet state then ensure it is idle
- Ensure fleet's warp is not on cool down
- Calculate warp distance, duration & expected fuel expenditure
- Check that the warp distance is within the fleet's warp range
- Set new warp cool down time
- Consume fuel
- Start the warp movement

definedTypes.name: singleCargo
docs: Represents a single type of cargo with its amount.
SingleCargo provides a simple structure for tracking a specific cargo type and its quantity.
It supports basic cargo operations like adding and removing cargo, as well as calculating
the storage cost based on the cargo type definition.
# System Context
This structure is used in contexts where a single cargo type needs to be tracked,
particularly for individual cargo transfers and storage cost calculations.
